text: The surgeon performs a right lower lobectomy, using a Maryland dissector with precise wrist movements to orient the instrument. A bipolar energy device is employed to coagulate small vessels, minimizing heat displacement. The inferior pulmonary vein is ligated first due to an incomplete fissure.
The surgeon at the bedside demonstrates the ease of switching instruments, such as changing from a Maryland dissector to a sucker or a standard diathermy hook, without relying on an assistant.

The surgeon precisely controls the Maryland dissector to follow tissue planes around the anterior hilum, while using fenestrated forceps in the other hand for blunt dissection around the vessel. The instruments provide tactile feedback, efficiently and safely preparing the path for stapling around the inferior pulmonary vein.
The surgeon introduces the stapler through a subxiphoid port, preparing to staple the inferior pulmonary vein during the right lower lobectomy procedure.
